texto: I would not have been able to articulate it at that time, but I had begun a painful journey toward an impossible goal, a journey that lasted a long time: how to love a God who hurts you.
explicacao: Citação de Carol Lynn Pearson sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “I would not have been able to articulate it at that time, but I had begun a painful journey towar…” articula fe, faith, god em registro de fe, com relevância para contextos como viagem e god.

frase_original: I would not have been able to articulate it at that time, but I had begun a painful journey toward an impossible goal, a journey that lasted a long time: how to love a God who hurts you.

texto: We can think a healed thought and speak a healed word, speak of and to the two who are One, our MotherGoddessFatherGod. The hopeful but misty thought that "I've a Mother there" will give way to the experience that "I've a Mother here." We will know Him, Her, Them, Us, the Divine Family unbroken, bringing part to whole and whole to part, singing the indispensable She who had been forgotten but it now found, singing the wholeness, singing the holiness.
explicacao: Citação de Carol Lynn Pearson sobre temas ligados a familia. “We can think a healed thought and speak a healed word, speak of and to the two who are One, our M…” articula familia, father, god em registro de familia, com relevância para contextos como god e goddess.

texto: I suggest that the representation of women deserves a much higher consideration in our religious discourse. When words are presented as if they come directly from God, they can have monumental impact on our psyches, our spirits, our hearts, and our relationships. Women are given, in story at least, first place in the lifeboats, but often in more common circumstances we are consigned to the back of the bus.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Carol Lynn Pearson: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “I suggest that the representation of women deserves a much higher consideration in our religious …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que fe, god, lds pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de lds e mormon.
